title: Expand bash command security validation to match CC depth (parity with bashCommandIsSafe_DEPRECATED)
description:
## Gap

CC's bashCommandIsSafe_DEPRECATED (tools/BashTool/bashSecurity.ts:2257-2420) applies ~15 security validators beyond OC's 16-pattern substring/regex denylist:

1. Control-character injection (non-printable chars that bypass validators)
2. Shell-quote single-quote backslash bug exploitation
3. IFS injection (validateIFSInjection, line 1017)
4. /proc/*/environ access (validateProcEnvironAccess, line 1041)
5. Carriage-return tokenization differential (shell-quote vs bash parse differently on CR)
6. Unicode whitespace smuggling (validateUnicodeWhitespace, line 1902)
7. Obfuscated flags (validateObfuscatedFlags, line 1130)
8. Brace expansion (validateBraceExpansion, line 1751)
9. Backslash-escaped operators (validateBackslashEscapedOperators, line 1696)
10. Mid-word hash comment injection (validateMidWordHash, line 1919)
11. Quoted-newline desync (validateCommentQuoteDesync, line 1990 / validateQuotedNewline, line 2109)
12. Process substitution >(...) / <(...) (pathValidation.ts:1028)
13. Windows UNC path injection (readOnlyValidation.ts:1903)
14. git + cd compound command sandbox escape (readOnlyValidation.ts:1917)
15. Malformed token injection (validateMalformedTokenInjection, line 1082)

OC only blocks 16 catastrophic fixed substrings + pipe-to-shell regex (src/tools/bash/policy.rs:89-133).

## Parity reference
- CC: tools/BashTool/bashSecurity.ts:2257-2420 (validator chain)
- OC: src/tools/bash/policy.rs:89-133 (minimal denylist only)

## Acceptance
- Implement the missing validators or a Rust equivalent of the validation chain
- Each validator has unit tests matching CC's test suite patterns
- Prefer AST-based analysis (see tree-sitter gap issue) over regex for structural patterns
